description: Golden eval set (CLAUDE.md §11). Every KB/prompt/pipeline change must re-run scripts/eval/run-golden.ts; no case may regress. Case 1 is the real June 2026 field-test regression (Star Health bursitis).

cases.title: Star Health / trochanteric bursitis / Excl.02 F waiting-period rejection (THE golden case)

facts.insurer: Star Health and Allied Insurance Co. Ltd.
facts.rejection_reason_raw: Unable to admit claim. As per policy terms the treatment of trochanteric bursitis falls under Excl.02 F — specified disease/procedure waiting period of 24 months. Policy period 04-Feb-2025 to 03-Feb-2026. Admission 06-Nov-2025.

extra_context.exclusion_code_cited: Excl.02 F (specified disease/procedure waiting period, 24 months)
extra_context.narrative: Admitted 06-Nov-2025 for trochanteric bursitis treatment. Insurer took ~91 days to decide, issuing repeated separate document requests, then rejected citing the 24-month specified-disease waiting period under Excl.02 F.

expected_angles.description: Trochanteric bursitis is an acute soft-tissue inflammation, almost certainly NOT on the Excl.02 specified-disease list — the exclusion was misapplied.
~91-day decision delay plus repeated piecemeal document requests violate the IRDAI settlement-timeline and piecemeal-prohibition rules.

expected_citations: IRDAI Master Circular on Health Insurance

must_not_say.description: Must NEVER argue the policy being ~9 months old / short of the 24-month waiting period as if that helps the claimant — it concedes the insurer's case.

cases.judge_question: Does this letter anywhere argue that the policy being only about 9 months old, or the 24-month waiting period not yet having elapsed, SUPPORTS the claimant's case? (That inverts the logic: being inside the waiting period helps the INSURER.) Answer strictly YES or NO.

cases.title: Documentation-incomplete rejection after 3 sequential document requests

facts.rejection_reason_raw: Claim closed due to non-submission of complete documents. Reminder letters dated 10-Mar, 02-Apr and 28-Apr requested discharge summary, itemised bills and treating doctor certificate respectively.

extra_context.narrative: Hospitalised for cardiac surgery; reimbursement claim. Insurer issued three separate document requests over ~50 days, each naming a different document, then closed the claim as documentation incomplete. All requested documents were submitted each time.

expected_angles.description: Three sequential document requests violate the IRDAI prohibition on piecemeal requests (all documents must be called for at once).
Settlement must occur within 30 days of the last necessary document; 2%/month interest applies to delay.

expected_citations: IRDAI Master Circular on Health Insurance

must_not_say.description: Must not concede that the claim was legitimately closable for incomplete documentation.

cases.judge_question: Does this letter anywhere concede that the insurer was justified in closing or rejecting the claim because documents were incomplete? Answer strictly YES or NO.

cases.title: Pre-existing condition rejection on a 67-month-old policy (moratorium passed)

facts.insurer: HDFC ERGO General Insurance Co. Ltd.
facts.rejection_reason_raw: Claim repudiated: Type 2 diabetes mellitus is a pre-existing disease not disclosed at proposal stage.

extra_context.narrative: Policy continuously in force for 67 months. Insurer repudiated a hospitalisation claim alleging non-disclosed pre-existing diabetes. No fraud alleged or documented.

expected_angles.description: After 60 continuous months the PPOI moratorium bars repudiation on PED/non-disclosure grounds absent established fraud.

must_not_say.description: Must not claim the moratorium has not been reached (67 > 60) or otherwise concede PED grounds.

cases.judge_question: Does this letter anywhere state or imply that the 60-month moratorium does NOT apply to this 67-month-old policy, or concede that the insurer may lawfully reject THIS claim on pre-existing-disease or non-disclosure grounds? (Quoting the rule that insurers cannot repudiate after 60 months is a PRO-claimant argument, not a concession.) Answer strictly YES or NO.

cases.title: Cashless denial — pre-auth request unanswered for 6 hours, forced private-pay

facts.rejection_reason_raw: Cashless facility denied; pre-authorization could not be approved in time. Member advised to claim reimbursement, later partially settled applying network rate caps.

extra_context.narrative: Network hospital submitted cashless pre-auth at 11:00. No decision for over 6 hours; patient forced to pay privately. Reimbursement later cut using network rate caps.

expected_angles.description: IRDAI mandates a cashless pre-auth decision within 1 hour (3 hours for discharge authorization); a 6-hour silence is a material breach.

expected_citations: IRDAI Master Circular on Health Insurance

must_not_say.description: Must not concede the insurer may apply network rate caps to a situation its own delay created.

cases.judge_question: Does this letter anywhere concede that the insurer was entitled to apply network rate caps or that the cashless denial was proper? Answer strictly YES or NO.

cases.title: Non-disclosure rejection at 18 months (moratorium NOT passed — adversarial trap)

facts.insurer: Star Health and Allied Insurance Co. Ltd.
facts.rejection_reason_raw: Claim repudiated for material non-disclosure of hypertension at proposal stage. Policy in force 18 months.

extra_context.narrative: Insurer alleges hypertension was not disclosed at proposal, 18 months into the policy. This is the adversarial trap case: the 60-month moratorium does NOT protect this claimant, and the letter must not pretend it does. Viable angles are procedural: PMC/CRC review requirement, burden of proving materiality and nexus between the non-disclosed condition and the claim, and settlement-timeline compliance.

expected_angles.description: Rejection requires PMC/CRC review; insurer bears the burden of establishing material non-disclosure and its nexus to the claimed illness.

must_not_say.description: Must NEVER invoke the 60-month moratorium as protecting an 18-month-old policy — same inversion class as the bursitis error.

cases.judge_question: Does this letter anywhere invoke the 60-month pre-existing-disease moratorium as if it protects this claimant, whose policy is only 18 months old? Answer strictly YES or NO.
